description: The AI digest Telegram layout refresh is tracked as its own harness task so future sessions resume from the current narrowed source policy and daily-layout-only scope instead of the completed coding-task lifecycle work.
steps: Open PLAN.json and verify it tracks only the AI digest daily Telegram layout refresh rather than the prior coding-task harness.
Open PROGRESS.md and verify it records that source narrowing is already complete and that this task only changes the daily Telegram digest presentation.
Confirm the baseline explicitly keeps weekly digest formatting, archive-cache behavior, Telegram renderer internals, and source-whitelist policy out of scope.

description: The daily Telegram digest starts with an explicit `AI 日报｜日期` title so the message reads as a real daily report instead of a bare conclusion card.
steps: Generate a daily Telegram digest and verify the first visible line is a bold `AI 日报｜YYYY-MM-DD` title.
Verify the title uses Telegram-safe markdown that renders cleanly through the existing Markdown-to-HTML conversion path.
Confirm the old conclusion-card style without a report title is no longer the default daily output.

description: The daily Telegram digest keeps the reporting window directly under the title, but removes extra intro copy so the message gets to the report quickly.
steps: Generate a daily Telegram digest and verify it shows the explicit reporting window immediately below the `AI 日报` title.
Verify there is no extra opening paragraph, emoji banner, or explanatory preface before the report body begins.
Confirm the reporting window still uses absolute timestamps with timezone.

description: The daily Telegram digest body uses a fixed four-quadrant report structure with `值得深挖`, `值得试试`, and `知道即可` sections rendered as readable markdown blocks.
steps: Generate a daily Telegram digest and verify the body contains the fixed section order for `值得深挖｜近 + 长`, `值得试试｜近 + 短`, and `知道即可｜远 + 长`.
Verify each section renders with bold headings, short bullets, and inline source links instead of long prose or plain text walls.
Confirm process-only sections such as `本轮未纳入` and `今日可忽略` do not appear in the Telegram daily digest.

description: The daily Telegram `今日结论` is attention-controlled by default, using one sentence in ordinary runs and expanding only to two short bullets when multiple same-level conclusions are genuinely important.
steps: Generate a normal daily Telegram digest and verify `今日结论` is a single sentence rather than a long paragraph or multiple loose bullets.
Generate or replay a day with multiple same-level top conclusions and verify `今日结论` can expand to two short bullets but not beyond that.
Confirm `今日结论` never regresses to three-or-more bullets or a long editorial intro.

description: Quadrant sections use a dynamic soft cap: one item by default, two when the same quadrant contains another clearly co-equal high-signal item, and at most three on rare breakout days.
steps: Replay a normal digest day and verify each quadrant defaults to a single item when there is no co-equal second item.
Replay a digest day with two clearly co-equal items in the same quadrant and verify that quadrant expands to two items.
Verify no quadrant exceeds three items and that empty quadrants say `今天无高信号项` rather than padding with noise.

description: The daily Telegram digest ends with a single-line `今日动作` that stays short and editorial, rather than turning into a task list or disappearing entirely.
steps: Generate a daily Telegram digest and verify it ends with a `今日动作` section containing exactly one short action line.
Verify the action line feels like a report takeaway or next read, not a multi-step task checklist.
Confirm the old English `Today action` label no longer appears in the daily Telegram output.

description: The Telegram daily markdown is shaped for the current Markdown-to-HTML renderer using bold headings, short bullets, and inline links instead of relying on hash headings or nested list structures.
steps: Generate a daily Telegram digest and verify bold headings survive rendering while the message remains easy to scan in Telegram.
Verify the digest does not rely on `#` heading syntax, tables, nested lists, or long quote blocks that collapse poorly in the current renderer.
Confirm inline links and short bullets render correctly through the existing Telegram HTML send path.

description: The daily digest skill contract and the active daily cron prompt are aligned, with both describing the same daily-report title, dynamic caps, concise conclusion behavior, and single-line action close.
steps: Open the active ai-news-digest SKILL.md and the daily digest cron prompt and verify both describe the same `AI 日报` daily Telegram structure.
Verify both sources describe the same dynamic soft-cap rule for quadrant items and the same default-versus-expanded `今日结论` behavior.
Confirm neither the skill nor the daily cron prompt still says the Telegram daily digest is a strict conclusion card or that each quadrant is hard-capped at one item.

description: Replay and sample validation prove the refreshed daily Telegram digest is clearer, shorter in conclusion density, and still able to surface multiple same-quadrant high-signal items without regressing archive behavior.
steps: Replay the last two daily archive samples into the new Telegram summary format and verify the result reads as a real daily report with title, quadrants, concise conclusion, and single action.
Verify the replay keeps important same-quadrant items when they are truly co-equal instead of forcing them out under a hard one-item cap.
Confirm the full archive markdown and cache-first reread behavior remain unchanged after the daily Telegram format refresh.
